title: Update working memory from favorites navigation session
summary: Durable context learned while unifying IPTVnator favorites and recently-viewed views, including scope behavior, route ownership, navigation decisions, and loading/layout conventions.

flows.name: Unified favorites and recently-viewed collection flow

claims.text: `UnifiedCollectionItem` is the shared display model for favorites and recently-viewed entries from M3U, Xtream, and Stalker sources, and it carries the content type (`live`, `movie`, or `series`) plus source-specific playback/navigation fields.

`UnifiedCollectionPageComponent` drives both favorites and recently-viewed modes, derives playlist context by walking ancestor route params, shows the playlist/all scope toggle only when a playlist id exists, and treats query-param `scope` as the highest-priority scope override before route `defaultScope` and persisted per-view scope.

Unified collection data uses `UnifiedFavoritesDataService` and `UnifiedRecentDataService`: playlist scope dispatches to playlist-specific M3U/Xtream/Stalker loaders when a playlist id is present, while all scope aggregates across all supported source types.

Xtream still has two global favorites queries: `getGlobalFavorites` keeps the legacy live-only `content.type = 'live'` filter, while `getAllGlobalFavorites` removes that filter and is the query used by unified favorites to include live TV, movies, and series.

Xtream and Stalker favorites/recent routes use lightweight collection route wrapper components that pass route-bound `mode` and `portalType` inputs into `UnifiedCollectionPageComponent`; workspace-level `global-favorites` and `global-recent` routes load the same page with `defaultScope: 'all'`.
M3U playlist favorites and recent routes remain on `M3uCollectionRouteComponent` instead of the unified Xtream/Stalker collection wrappers.

The unified collection page uses a header content-type segmented control and `@switch` rendering: live entries go through `UnifiedLiveTabComponent` with the reusable global-favorites channel list, EPG, playback, and drag reorder, while movie and series entries go through `UnifiedGridTabComponent` poster cards and detail navigation.

Unified collections no longer use a Material progress spinner for initial loading; they share the extracted skeleton SCSS primitives with `grid-list`, rendering row skeletons for live lists and poster-card skeletons for movies/series.

`parseWorkspaceShellRoute` treats portal favorites and recent routes as local-filter/searchable collection routes with no context sidebar; the collection context panel is reserved for portal downloads, while category sidebars stay on Xtream/Stalker VOD, series, and live/ITV sections.

The workspace global-favorites header action is smart-routed: when an Xtream or Stalker rail context exists it navigates to that portal's `favorites` route with `?scope=all`, otherwise it falls back to `/workspace/global-favorites`; portal favorites with `scope=all` also count as the active global-favorites header state.

The unified collection UX should use two compact segmented controls in one header row, one for content type and one for playlist/all scope; the session rejected keeping the content-type selector as centered Material tabs or as a separate left filter sidebar for these views.

Favorites and Recently Viewed links should remain in the portal rail as contextual navigation, while the header star stays as an all-favorites shortcut into the active portal's favorites route with all scope; the session explicitly avoided removing the rail links or adding Recently Viewed as another header shortcut.

The unification scope intentionally leaves M3U favorites/recent navigation on its existing M3U collection/player flow because M3U has only live-TV channel collections and does not need the Xtream/Stalker movie/series content-type split.

Revisit the standalone `/workspace/global-favorites` fallback UX for cases with no active portal context; the session left open whether that workspace-level route should simply aggregate everything or add stronger portal/context selection affordances.

metadata.reason: The user asked whether tabs should move into the header or become segmented controls, and the accepted implementation replaced tabs with a same-row content-type toggle beside the scope toggle.

The user asked for a no-implementation plan about rail versus header favorites/recent navigation, approved the recommendation, and the session implemented the header star as a global-first shortcut while keeping rail links.
The brainstorming constraints and accepted UX plan called out M3U playlists as live-only and kept them separate from the unified Xtream/Stalker collection treatment.
The approved navigation plan kept `/workspace/global-favorites` as a fallback when no portal context is active and explicitly noted that its standalone UX could be reconsidered.
